prestazioni: Engine swap to a Jaguar XK6 4.2L (engine code: XK6 4.2L E-Type) with forged internals including Mahle Motorsport forged pistons (9.5:1 compression), Cunningham conrods, and a billet crankshaft from JE Motorsport. Pair with a custom-built TWR (Tom Walkinshaw Racing) cylinder head featuring oversized 2.14" intake and 1.65" exhaust valves, ported and polished by Hartwell Racing.

Forced induction via a Rotrex C38-91 supercharger (12 psi boost) with a custom intercooler setup using a PWR Racing heat exchanger and custom-fabricated 3" aluminum piping. Fuel system upgrade includes dual Bosch 044 high-flow fuel pumps, Injector Dynamics ID1700x injectors, and a Haltech Elite 2500 ECU with a custom tune by Bell Auto Services (UK).

Drivetrain reinforcement with a Quaife ATB differential (3.54:1 ratio) in a Jaguar XJ-S limited-slip housing, paired with a Tilton twin-plate ceramic clutch and a Getrag 265 5-speed manual transmission (from a Jaguar XJ-S) with a custom bellhousing adapter by RPI Engineering. Suspension overhaul with Ohlins Road & Track DFV coilovers (custom valving for Mark 2 chassis), polygraphite bushings from SuperPro, and a full chassis reinforcement kit by SNG Barratt.
estetica: Full custom widebody conversion using hand-formed aluminum panels by RS Panels (UK), replicating the 1960s Jaguar Mark 2 rally cars with flared fenders (2.5" wider front, 3" wider rear) and integrated side skirts. Rear quarter windows replaced with lightweight Lexan panels by Sabelt.

Custom front bumper delete with a hand-fabricated aluminum grille surround (brushed finish) and Marchal 180 headlights (H4 conversion) with custom Cibié H1 fog lights integrated into the lower valence. Rear bumper replaced with a custom aluminum unit (polished) featuring integrated twin exhaust exits for a 3" stainless steel system by Hayward & Scott.

Interior retrim in full Connolly leather (Bridge of Weir hide) with diamond-quilted door cards and rear seat panels, custom center console housing a Momo Prototipo steering wheel (380mm) on a NRG quick-release hub, and a full digital dash conversion using a Motec C127 display with vintage-inspired analog-style gauges. Floor-mounted pedals by Tilton and a custom roll cage by Safety Devices (UK) with integrated harness bar for Schroth 6-point harnesses.
